comments.body: Go to your Update Sites list and clean it, keep only the official Neon sites and disable everything else. Also, check your Preferences -> Network
Nope, disabled all of the Juno sites and still didn't work. I also set the Active Connection to Manual and cleared SOCKS.
Did you try to fetch marketplace.eclipse.org/catalogs/api/p in your browser? I get the catalog XML. What do you get?
Is your firewall preventing Eclipse from talking to marketplace.eclipse.org?
@Jim Garrison When I go to the marketplace site, I just see a bunch of HTML. Is that supposed to happen?
@discipline I turned firewall completely off, still not working.
it's XML not HTML, but that is what you should see, which means you are connecting to the server.
@SSung2710 turn off your anti-virus and try again. Also, try using the IPv4 Java environment variable -Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true

answers.body: Turning off the anti-virus worked for me.

you should change proxy setting in eclipse:

  1. Windows --> Preferences --> General --> Network Connection
  2. Change 'Active Provider' to Manual

title: Cannot open Eclipse Marketplace
body:
I'm trying to open the Eclipse Marketplace from Eclipse Neon 64-bit. Whenever I try to select Help-> Eclipse Marketplace, I get the following message:

Cannot open Eclipse Marketplace

Cannot install remote marketplace locations: Connection failed

In the error log:
